nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as time-consuming or inefficient tool
nodes.grounding_span: AI fits into my regular workday in many ways. As a teacher, I rely on AI to help me save time and mental expenditure for administrative tasks such as writing emails, crafting replies to emails, brainstorming ideas for lessons or units.

nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as non-collaborative or directive tool
nodes.grounding_span: I often use AI as a collaborative partner or an administrative assistant helping me save time or bounce ideas off of when I am feeling mentally drained and in the depths of decision fatigue.

nodes.label: AI as an external memory aid
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary crutch that replaces personal knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: one difficulty is just remembering them all. There are so many teaching ideas out there, it is nice to have someone - AI in this case - remind you of all the options so you aren't exhausting yourself looking back into your notes or the depths of the web to recall them all.

nodes.label: AI as tool for lesson planning
nodes.label_negative: AI as rigid or uncreative lesson planner
nodes.grounding_span: I used AI to help me plan different creative solutions and adaptations to my current unit to help ensure I am accurately addressing the provincial curricular outcomes while also incorporating a fresh perspective

nodes.label: AI as tool to address academic dishonesty
nodes.label_negative: AI as enabler of academic dishonesty
nodes.grounding_span: asking AI to reword a multiple choice exam and rearrange the questions to create another version (to cut down on academic dishonesty)

nodes.label: AI as tool for lesson planning
nodes.label_negative: AI as shortcut that undermines student learning
nodes.grounding_span: I had this idea where I asked him to rate three paragraphs. One paragraph was his own, but I asked AI to rewrite the same paragraph with the same content at each of the other rubric levels. This really got through to him

nodes.label: AI use in classroom management and interpersonal decisions
nodes.label_negative: AI avoidance in classroom management and interpersonal decisions
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to handle those personally. I trust my instincts, professional judgement and extensive experience to respond in a way that feels authentic for me.

nodes.label: AI integration in teaching profession
nodes.label_negative: AI rejection or skepticism in teaching profession
nodes.grounding_span: I would love to see the relationship with AI develop and become fully integrated into part of our workload for teaching.

nodes.label: Teacher efficiency and reduced burnout
nodes.grounding_span: teachers are asked to do an obscene amount of work within very limited paid hours and honestly, its so overwhelming and discouraging that so many teachers end up with premature burnout

nodes.label: Teacher authenticity and professional identity
nodes.grounding_span: I think that is key for developing a strong rapport with your students - it really requires developing your instincts and also showing them your human emotions.

nodes.label: Critical thinking and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: allows my students to be creative and practice critical thinking skills

nodes.label: Student well-being and mental health
nodes.grounding_span: as someone who takes mental health and student success very seriously, I felt it was my responsibility to ensure I take that factor into account

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for time-saving
nodes.grounding_span: I rely on AI to help me save time and mental expenditure

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for brainstorming
nodes.grounding_span: I often use AI as a collaborative partner or an administrative assistant helping me save time or bounce ideas off of

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as memory aid
nodes.grounding_span: it is nice to have someone - AI in this case - remind you of all the options

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for lesson creation
nodes.grounding_span: I used AI to help me plan different creative solutions and adaptations

nodes.label: Frustration with AI dishonesty
nodes.grounding_span: asking AI to reword a multiple choice exam and rearrange the questions to create another version (to cut down on academic dishonesty)

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: This really got through to him and helped him both see what specific improvements he could make

nodes.label: Frustration with student AI misuse
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to handle those personally. I trust my instincts, professional judgement and extensive experience

nodes.label: Cautious optimism about AI in education
nodes.grounding_span: I would love to see the relationship with AI develop and become fully integrated into part of our workload for teaching.

nodes.label: Task-specific interaction pattern adaptation
nodes.grounding_span: I would say both of those occurred throughout the conversation, but mainly I presented my ideas and suggestions upfront for the AI tool to prioritize during the conversation but later on, when I realized that I had to adapt a bit to modify one of my assignments to address a curriculuar outcome I had initially forgotten, I asked it to present me with some supplementary suggestions

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting — clear separation of AI-appropriate vs. human-only tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to handle those personally. I trust my instincts, professional judgement and extensive experience to respond in a way that feels authentic for me.
